
### Batch, None

```{r parameters_and_start_up_batch_none}

# preparing to make new files for the new data processing method
file_added_name = '_batch_none' 

# Using the flexibility from the previous code, these parameters will be updated for the batch, none run
final_param = TRUE

if(final_param){
    b6_correction <- FALSE
    batch_as_covar <- TRUE
    rank_norm <- FALSE
    log_trans <- FALSE
    weight_loss <- TRUE
}
#===============================================================

if(is.na(b6_correction) || length(b6_correction) == 0){
b6_correction = FALSE; batch_as_covar = FALSE; rank_norm = FALSE
}

if(b6_correction && batch_as_covar){stop("Set either b6_correction or batch_as_covar to TRUE, not both.")}

alpha <- c(0.15, 0.2)

overwrite_previous <- FALSE # make this true whenever you add new data that you want fully analyzed; this WILL take longer and may not work on a low powered machine
add_founder_genotypes <- FALSE

#strains identified by remove_strains are excluded from all
#manipulations including normalization and batch corrections.
#justficiations are as follows:
#CC027 - all but 2 were homozygous for the wrong genotype at the H2 locus
#NOD and 129 - are not used for mapping, and were not included in all
#batches, so we cannot use them for normalization
#remove_strains <- NULL
remove_strains <- c("CC027", "NOD", "129")
#remove_strains <- c("CC051", "CC059") #closely related
#remove_strains <- c("CC051") #pick one of them to keep

cc.color.scheme = "original"
#cc.color.scheme = "new"

#for calculations
exclude.zeros = FALSE #if true, we exclude animals with a CDS of 0
disease.thresh = 0 #maximum disease score considered disease-free

nperm = 1000

if(nperm > 0){
    run_permutations <- TRUE
}else{
    run_permutations <- FALSE
}

if(add_founder_genotypes){
    founders.included = "yes"
}else{
    founders.included = "no"
}

if(length(remove_strains) > 0){
    strains.removed <- paste(remove_strains, collapse = ", ")
}else{
    strains.removed = "none"
}

if(!exclude.zeros){
    zero.text <- "Zeros are included in CDS"
    zero.treatment <- "included with"
}else{
    zero.text <- paste("Animals with scores of", disease.thresh, "and below are excluded from CDS.")
    zero.treatment <- "excluded from"
}

if(b6_correction){
    correction.text <- "Batch corrections were made using B6 CDS."
    correction.label <- "B6-batch"
}
if(batch_as_covar){
    correction.text <- "Batch corrections were done using batch as a covariate."
    correction.label <- "covar-batch"
}
if(!b6_correction && !batch_as_covar){
    correction.text <- "No batch corrections were done."
    correction.label <- "no-batch"
}

if(rank_norm){
    norm.text <- "Rank Z normalization was performed."
    norm.label <- "rank-norm"
}else if (log_trans){
    norm.text <- "Log Transformation was performed"
    norm.label <- 'log-trans'
}else{
    norm.text <- "Rank Z normalization was not performed."
    norm.label <- "no-norm"
}

file.label <- paste(correction.label, norm.label, genotypes, sep = "_")
```

The parameters for this run are as follows:

* Genotypes used: `r genotypes`

* Founders included: `r founders.included`

* CC Strains removed: `r strains.removed`

* Treatment of zeros: `r zero.text`

* Batch correction: `r correction.text`

* Normalization: `r norm.text`

```{r reload_data_batch_none}
if(cc.color.scheme == "original"){
    strain.colors <- CCorigcolors
}else{
    strain.colors <- CCcolors
}

cds <- read.csv(here("Data", paste0("CDS_", cds.file.date, ".csv")), strip.white = TRUE)
histopath <- read.csv(here("Data", paste0("histopath_", histopath.file.date, ".csv")), strip.white = TRUE)

#take out any slashes in strain names
cds[,"Strain"]  <- gsub("/", "", cds[,"Strain"], fixed = TRUE)
histopath[,"Strain"]  <- gsub("/", "", histopath[,"Strain"], fixed = TRUE)

#since Average Weight Loss was measured by simply taking the average of all the weight deltas weight loss is negative and gain is positive
#however this results in an inverted correlation and QTL plot between weight Loss and CDS despite both being metrics of disease severity
#below we can Loss average weight Loss to be negative and thus have CDS and Weight Loss correlate positively and map together
if(weight_loss){
  weight_data.text <- "Average Weight Loss reflects negative values for weight gain and positive values for weight loss"
  cds['Avg.Weight.Loss'] = cds['Avg.Weight.Loss'] *(-1)
}else{
  weight_data.text <- "Average Weight Loss reflects positive values for weight gain and negative values for weight loss."
}
```

```{r remove_strains_batch_none}
#remove any empty rows that were inserted to visually separate
#the founders from the rest of the strains
cds.data.idx <- which(!is.na(cds[,"ID"]))
cds <- cds[cds.data.idx,]

histopath.data.idx <- which(!is.na(histopath[,"ID"]))
histopath <- histopath[histopath.data.idx,]

#remove specified strains from cds
if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.cds <- unlist(sapply(remove_strains, function(x) which(cds[,"Strain"] == x)))
    cds <- cds[-remove.cds,]
}

if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.histopath <- unlist(sapply(remove_strains, function(x) which(histopath[,"Strain"] == x)))
    histopath <- histopath[histopath.data.idx,]
}
```


```{r batches_batch_none}
## CDS
num.cds <- as.matrix(sapply(cds[,c("Classical.CDS", "AR.CDS", "Global.CDS", "Avg.Weight.Loss")], as.numeric)) #had to change this with Chat to make the values numeric
rownames(num.cds) <- cds[,"ID"]
#set all scores equal to or lower than the disease threshold
#to zero
if(exclude.zeros){
    num.cds[which(num.cds <= disease.thresh)] <- 0
}

factor.cds <- as.matrix(cds[,c("Strain", "Sex")])
u_batch <- unique(cds[,"EXP"])

#remove animals in batch 170 - these were validation, not part of the original experiment
to.remove = "X170"
keep.batch <- which(u_batch != "X170")
u_batch <- u_batch[keep.batch]

keep.idx <- which(cds[,"EXP"] != "X170")
cds <- cds[keep.idx,]
num.cds <- num.cds[keep.idx,]
factor.cds <- factor.cds[keep.idx,]

batch.mat <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(cds), ncol = length(u_batch))
colnames(batch.mat) <- u_batch
for(b in 1:ncol(batch.mat)){
    batch.mat[which(cds[,"EXP"] == u_batch[b]),b] <- 1
}
cds.covar <- as.matrix(cbind(batch.mat, cds[,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE]))

pheno.strains <- cds[,"Strain"]
eae.strains <- unique(pheno.strains)

## histopath
# saving original histopath
histopath_legacy <- histopath

# removing animals that don't yet have histopathology scores
histopath <- na.omit(histopath)
removed_strains_histopath <- setdiff(unique(cds$Strain),unique(histopath$Strain)) #seeing what strains histopathology still lacks

# continuing with variable creation like above
num.histopath <- as.matrix(sapply(histopath[,c("H.E.SC.Overall", "H.E.BN.Overall", "LFB.SC.Overall", "LFB.BN.Overall")], as.numeric)) #had to change this with Chat to make the values numeric
rownames(num.histopath) <- histopath[,"ID"]
#set all scores equal to or lower than the disease threshold
#to zero
if(exclude.zeros){
    num.histopath[which(num.histopath <= disease.thresh)] <- 0
}

factor.histopath <- as.matrix(histopath[,c("Strain", "Sex")])
u_batch_histopath <- unique(histopath[,"EXP"])

#remove animals in batch 170 - these were validation, not part of the original experiment
to.remove = "X170"
keep_histopath.batch_histopath <- which(u_batch_histopath != "X170")
u_batch_histopath <- u_batch_histopath[keep_histopath.batch_histopath]

keep_histopath.idx <- which(histopath[,"EXP"] != "X170")
histopath <- histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]
num.histopath <- num.histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]
factor.histopath <- factor.histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]

batch_histopath.mat <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(histopath), ncol = length(u_batch_histopath))
colnames(batch_histopath.mat) <- u_batch_histopath
for(b in 1:ncol(batch_histopath.mat)){
    batch_histopath.mat[which(histopath[,"EXP"] == u_batch_histopath[b]),b] <- 1
}
histopath.covar <- as.matrix(cbind(batch_histopath.mat, histopath[,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE]))

pheno_histopath.strains_histopath <- histopath[,"Strain"]
eae_histopath.strains_histopath <- unique(pheno_histopath.strains_histopath)

# getting a histopath subset index
histopath_strain_names = unique(histopath$Strain)
```

```{r adj_cds_batch_none}
#choose which phenotypes to batch
#correct using their indices.
cds.idx <- c(1,2,3,4)
adj.cds <- num.cds

if(batch_as_covar){
    adj.cds <- adjust(num.cds, cds.covar[,1:ncol(cds.covar)-1])
}
```

```{r adj_histopath_batch_none}
#choose which phenotypes to batch
#correct using their indices.
histopath.idx <- c(1,2,3,4)

adj.histopath <- num.histopath

if(batch_as_covar){
    adj.histopath <- adjust(num.histopath, histopath.covar[,1:ncol(histopath.covar)-1])
}

```

```{r adjust_age_cds_batch_none, eval = FALSE}
#adjust for age
for(i in 1:ncol(adj.cds)){
    if(exclude.zeros){
        nonzero.idx <- which(num.cds[,i] != 0)
    }else{
        nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.cds)
    }
    adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i] <- adjust(adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE],
        cds.covar[nonzero.idx,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE])
}

#write out the results of whatever adjustments we have done
write.table(adj.cds, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Adj_CDS", file_added_name, ".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)
```

```{r no_norm_cds_batch_none}
norm.cds <- adj.cds
colnames(norm.cds) <- gsub(".CDS", "", colnames(norm.cds)) #take off CDS in labels, to save room in plots

if(rank_norm){
    for(i in 1:ncol(norm.cds)){
        if(exclude.zeros){
            nonzero.idx <- which(num.cds[,i] != 0)
        }else{
            nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.cds)
        }
        norm.cds[nonzero.idx,i] <- rankZ(adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i])
    }
}
colnames(norm.cds) <- gsub(".CDS", "", colnames(norm.cds)) #take off CDS, to save room in plots

#write out normzliaed values
write.table(norm.cds, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Norm_CDS",file_added_name,".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)

```

```{r adjust_age_histopath_batch_none, eval = FALSE}
#adjust for age
for(i in 1:ncol(adj.histopath)){
    if(exclude.zeros){
        nonzero.idx <- which(num.histopath[,i] != 0)
    }else{
        nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.histopath)
    }
    adj.histopath[nonzero.idx,i] <- adjust(adj.histopath[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE],
        histopath.covar[nonzero.idx,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE])
}

#write out the results of whatever adjustments we have done
write.table(adj.histopath, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Adj_histopath",file_added_name,".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)


#colnames(norm.histopath) <- gsub(".histopath", "", colnames(norm.histopath)) #take off CDS in labels, to save room in plots; not relavent now
```

```{r norm_histopath_batch_none}
norm.histopath <- adj.histopath
if(rank_norm){
    for(i in 1:ncol(norm.histopath)){
        if(exclude.zeros){
            nonzero.idx <- which(num.histopath[,i] != 0)
        }else{
            nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.histopath)
        }
        norm.histopath[nonzero.idx,i] <- rankZ(adj.histopath[nonzero.idx,i])
    }
}
#colnames(norm.histopath) <- gsub(".histopath", "", colnames(norm.histopath)) #take off CDS in labels, to save room in plots; not relevant now

#write out normzliaed values
write.table(norm.histopath, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Norm_histopath",file_added_name, ".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)

```

```{r methods_batch_none}
if(add_founder_genotypes){
    methods.line <- "Both CC and founder strains were used for mapping."
}else{
    methods.line <- "We used only CC strains for mapping."
}

if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.line <- paste("We excluded the following strains:", paste(remove_strains, collapse = ", "))
}else{
    remove.line <- "No CC strains were excluded."
}

if(length(removed_strains_histopath) !=0){
  remove.line <- paste("The strains that have CDS data but not yet histopathology data are:", paste(removed_strains_histopath, collapse = ", "))
}else{
  remove.line <- "All strains have both CDS and histopathology data."
}

#making histopath properly dimensioned for expanded matrices
if (!add_founder_genotypes){
  histopath_mod_legacy <- histopath_legacy[!(histopath_legacy$Strain %in% c('NOD','129')),] #didn't remove B6 because these are still used for other things
}
founder_ids <-
if (!add_founder_genotypes){
  norm.histopath <- norm.histopath[!(histopath$Strain %in% c('C57BL6','NOD','129')),]
}

```

`r methods.line`
`r remove.line`

```{r expand_matrices_cds_batch_none}

#remove any strains not used in mapping
mapping.strain.idx <- unlist(sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, pheno.strains)))
pheno.strains_cds <- pheno.strains[mapping.strain.idx]

#make a unique ID for each mouse that contains its strain and sex
u_id <- as.vector(apply(cds[mapping.strain.idx,c(1:2,5)], 1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = "_")))

K.idx <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, rownames(mapping.K[[1]])))
K.num <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) length(which(pheno.strains_cds == x)))
expanded.idx <- unlist(lapply(1:length(K.idx), function(x) rep(K.idx[x], K.num[x])))
groups <- names(expanded.idx)

exp.K <- lapply(1:length(mapping.K), function(x) mapping.K[[x]][expanded.idx,expanded.idx])
names(exp.K) <- names(mapping.K)

#create unique row and column names
for(i in 1:length(exp.K)){
    rownames(exp.K[[i]]) <- colnames(exp.K[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id)
}

expanded.genoprobs <- mapping.genoprobs
for(i in 1:length(expanded.genoprobs)){
    expanded.genoprobs[[i]] <- mapping.genoprobs[[i]][expanded.idx,,]
    rownames(expanded.genoprobs[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id)
}

#align the phenotypes with the strains
covar <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(cds[mapping.strain.idx,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(covar) <- 1:length(u_id)

mapping.pheno <- norm.cds[mapping.strain.idx,]
rownames(mapping.pheno) <- 1:length(u_id)

mapping.factors <- factor.cds[mapping.strain.idx,]
mapping.covar <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(mapping.factors[,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(mapping.covar) <- 1:length(u_id)
```

```{r expand_matrices_histopath_batch_none}

#remove any strains not used in mapping
mapping.strain.idx_histopath <- unlist(sapply(mapping.strains[histopath_mapping_idx], function(x) grep(x, pheno_histopath.strains_histopath)))
pheno.strains_histopath <- pheno_histopath.strains_histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath]

#make a unique ID for each mouse that contains its strain and sex
u_id_histopath <- as.vector(apply(histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath,c(1:2,5)],1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = "_")))

K.idx_histopath <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, rownames(mapping.K[[1]])))
K.num_histopath <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) length(which(pheno.strains_histopath == x)))
expanded.idx_histopath <- unlist(lapply(1:length(K.idx_histopath), function(x) rep(K.idx_histopath[x], K.num_histopath[x])))
groups_histopath <- names(expanded.idx_histopath)

exp.K_histopath <- lapply(1:length(mapping.K), function(x) mapping.K[[x]][expanded.idx_histopath,expanded.idx_histopath])
names(exp.K_histopath) <- names(mapping.K)

#create unique row and column names
for(i in 1:length(exp.K_histopath)){
    rownames(exp.K_histopath[[i]]) <- colnames(exp.K_histopath[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)
}

expanded.genoprobs_histopath <- mapping.genoprobs
for(i in 1:length(expanded.genoprobs_histopath)){
    expanded.genoprobs_histopath[[i]] <- mapping.genoprobs[[i]][expanded.idx_histopath,,]
    rownames(expanded.genoprobs_histopath[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)
}

#align the phenotypes with the strains
covar_histopath <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(covar_histopath) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)

mapping.pheno_histopath <- norm.histopath
rownames(mapping.pheno_histopath) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)

mapping.factors_histopath <- factor.histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath,]
mapping.covar_histopath <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(mapping.factors_histopath[,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(mapping.covar_histopath) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)
```


```{r herit_cds_batch_none, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 5}
overall.k.file <- here("Data",paste0("kinship_overall_cds",file_added_name,".RDS"))
if(!file.exists(overall.k.file) || overwrite_previous){
    overall.K <- calc_kinship(expanded.genoprobs, "overall")
    saveRDS(overall.K, overall.k.file)
}else{
    overall.K <- readRDS(overall.k.file)
}

ind.herit <- rep(NA, ncol(num.cds))
names(ind.herit) <- colnames(num.cds)
for(i in 1:ncol(num.cds)){
    nonzero.idx <- which(mapping.pheno[,i] != 0)
    ind.herit[i] <- est_herit(mapping.pheno[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE], overall.K)
}


```

```{r herit_histopath_batch_none, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 5}
overall_histopath.k.file <- here("Data", paste0("kinship_overall_histopath",file_added_name,".RDS"))
if(!file.exists(overall_histopath.k.file) || overwrite_previous){
    overall_histopath.K <- calc_kinship(expanded.genoprobs_histopath, "overall")
    saveRDS(overall_histopath.K, overall_histopath.k.file)
}else{
    overall_histopath.K <- readRDS(overall_histopath.k.file)
}

ind.herit_histopath <- rep(NA, ncol(num.histopath))
names(ind.herit_histopath) <- colnames(num.histopath)
for(i in 1:ncol(num.histopath)){
    nonzero.idx <- which(mapping.pheno[,i] != 0)
    ind.herit_histopath[i] <- est_herit(mapping.pheno[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE], overall_histopath.K)
}


```

```{r map_cds_batch_none, warning = FALSE}

cds.scans <- map_pheno(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, exp.K,
    addcovar = covar, exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros,results.file = file.path(results.dir, paste0("scan1_results_cds",file_added_name,".RDS")))



if(run_permutations){
    #perm_strata <- names(expanded.idx)
    #names(perm_strata) <- rownames(mapping.pheno)
    #cds.perm <- scan1perm(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, kinship = exp.K,
    #    addcovar = covar, perm_strata = perm_strata, n_perm = nperm, cores = detectCores()-1)

    #cds.perm <- scan1perm(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, kinship = exp.K,
    #    addcovar = covar, n_perm = nperm, cores = detectCores()-1)

    cds.perm <- perm_scans(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, exp.K,
        cds.scans, addcovar = covar, groups = names(expanded.idx),
        exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros, nperm = nperm,
        results.file = file.path(results.dir, paste0("scan1perm_results_cds_perm_scans",file_added_name,".RDS")))
}
```

```{r map_histopath_batch_none, warning=FALSE}

histopath.scans <- map_pheno(expanded.genoprobs_histopath, mapping.pheno_histopath, exp.K_histopath,
    addcovar = covar_histopath, exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros,results.file = file.path(results.dir, paste0("scan1_results_histopath",file_added_name,".RDS")))



if(run_permutations){
    #perm_strata <- names(expanded.idx)
    #names(perm_strata) <- rownames(mapping.pheno)
    #cds.perm <- scan1perm(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, kinship = exp.K,
    #    addcovar = covar, perm_strata = perm_strata, n_perm = nperm, cores = detectCores()-1)

    #cds.perm <- scan1perm(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, kinship = exp.K,
    #    addcovar = covar, n_perm = nperm, cores = detectCores()-1)

    histopath.perm <- perm_scans(expanded.genoprobs_histopath, mapping.pheno_histopath, exp.K_histopath,
        histopath.scans, addcovar = covar_histopath, groups = names(expanded.idx_histopath),
        exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros, nperm = nperm,
        results.file = file.path(results.dir, paste0("scan1perm_results_histopath_perm_scans",file_added_name,".RDS")))
}
```

#### CDS by individual LOD traces for Batch, None {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r plot_cds_scans_batch_none, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4, warning = FALSE, results = "asis"}
#plot the scans, and FDR results
if(run_permutations){
    #pdf("~/Desktop/test.pdf")
    fdr_summaries_cds <- plot_fdr_fun(scan.results = cds.scans, perm.results = cds.perm,
        tab.level = "#####", maxy = NULL)
    #sig_summaries_cds <- plot_pval_fun(scan.results = cds.scans, perm.results = cds.perm, tab.level = "####")
    #dev.off()
}else{
    for(i in 1:length(cds.scans)){
        cat("#####", names(cds.scans)[i], "\n")
        plot(cds.scans[[i]], map = map)
        cat("\n\n")
    }
}
```

#### Histopathology by individual LOD traces for Batch, None {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r plot_histopath_scans_batch_none, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4, warning = FALSE, results = "asis"}
#plot the scans, and FDR results
if(run_permutations){
    #pdf("~/Desktop/test.pdf")
    fdr_summaries_histopath <- plot_fdr_fun(scan.results = histopath.scans, perm.results = histopath.perm,
        tab.level = "#####", maxy = NULL)
    #sig_summaries_cds <- plot_pval_fun(scan.results = cds.scans, perm.results = cds.perm, tab.level = "####")
    #dev.off()
}else{
    for(i in 1:length(histopath.scans)){
        cat("#####", names(histopath.scans)[i], "\n")
        plot(histopath.scans[[i]], map = map)
        cat("\n\n")
    }
}
```

#### LOD intervals for Batch, None {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r lod_peaks_cds_batch_none, results = "asis"}
top.n <- 3
for(ph in 1:length(cds.scans)){
    cat("#####", names(cds.scans)[ph], "\n")
    peak.table <- find_peaks(cds.scans[[ph]], map = map, prob = 0.95)
    lod.order <- order(peak.table[,"lod"], decreasing = TRUE)
    top.peaks <- peak.table[lod.order[1:top.n],3:ncol(peak.table)]
    rownames(top.peaks) <- NULL
    print(kable(top.peaks))
    cat("Thresholds:\n")
    print(kable(fdr_summaries_cds[ph,,drop=FALSE]))
    cat("\n\n")
}
```


```{r lod_peaks_histopath_batch_none, results = "asis"}
top.n <- 3
for(ph in 1:length(histopath.scans)){
    cat("#####", names(histopath.scans)[ph], "\n")
    peak.table_histopath <- find_peaks(histopath.scans[[ph]], map = map, prob = 0.95)
    lod.order_histopath <- order(peak.table_histopath[,"lod"], decreasing = TRUE)
    top.peaks_histopath <- peak.table_histopath[lod.order_histopath[1:top.n],3:ncol(peak.table_histopath)]
    rownames(top.peaks_histopath) <- NULL
    print(kable(top.peaks_histopath))
    cat("Thresholds:\n")
    print(kable(fdr_summaries_histopath[ph,,drop=FALSE]))
    cat("\n\n")
}
```
### None, Rank Z

```{r parameters_and_start_up_none_rank_z}

# preparing to make new files for the new data processing method
file_added_name = '_none_rank_z' 

# Using the flexibility from the previous code, these parameters will be updated for the none, rank Z run
final_param = TRUE

if(final_param){
    b6_correction <- FALSE
    batch_as_covar <- FALSE
    rank_norm <- TRUE
    log_trans <- FALSE
    weight_loss <- TRUE
}
#===============================================================

if(is.na(b6_correction) || length(b6_correction) == 0){
b6_correction = FALSE; batch_as_covar = FALSE; rank_norm = FALSE
}

if(b6_correction && batch_as_covar){stop("Set either b6_correction or batch_as_covar to TRUE, not both.")}

alpha <- c(0.15, 0.2)

overwrite_previous <- FALSE # make this true whenever you add new data that you want fully analyzed; this WILL take longer and may not work on a low powered machine
add_founder_genotypes <- FALSE

#strains identified by remove_strains are excluded from all
#manipulations including normalization and batch corrections.
#justficiations are as follows:
#CC027 - all but 2 were homozygous for the wrong genotype at the H2 locus
#NOD and 129 - are not used for mapping, and were not included in all
#batches, so we cannot use them for normalization
#remove_strains <- NULL
remove_strains <- c("CC027", "NOD", "129")
#remove_strains <- c("CC051", "CC059") #closely related
#remove_strains <- c("CC051") #pick one of them to keep

cc.color.scheme = "original"
#cc.color.scheme = "new"

#for calculations
exclude.zeros = FALSE #if true, we exclude animals with a CDS of 0
disease.thresh = 0 #maximum disease score considered disease-free

nperm = 1000

if(nperm > 0){
    run_permutations <- TRUE
}else{
    run_permutations <- FALSE
}

if(add_founder_genotypes){
    founders.included = "yes"
}else{
    founders.included = "no"
}

if(length(remove_strains) > 0){
    strains.removed <- paste(remove_strains, collapse = ", ")
}else{
    strains.removed = "none"
}

if(!exclude.zeros){
    zero.text <- "Zeros are included in CDS"
    zero.treatment <- "included with"
}else{
    zero.text <- paste("Animals with scores of", disease.thresh, "and below are excluded from CDS.")
    zero.treatment <- "excluded from"
}

if(b6_correction){
    correction.text <- "Batch corrections were made using B6 CDS."
    correction.label <- "B6-batch"
}
if(batch_as_covar){
    correction.text <- "Batch corrections were done using batch as a covariate."
    correction.label <- "covar-batch"
}
if(!b6_correction && !batch_as_covar){
    correction.text <- "No batch corrections were done."
    correction.label <- "no-batch"
}

if(rank_norm){
    norm.text <- "Rank Z normalization was performed."
    norm.label <- "rank-norm"
}else if (log_trans){
    norm.text <- "Log Transformation was performed"
    norm.label <- 'log-trans'
}else{
    norm.text <- "Rank Z normalization was not performed."
    norm.label <- "no-norm"
}

file.label <- paste(correction.label, norm.label, genotypes, sep = "_")
```

The parameters for this run are as follows:

* Genotypes used: `r genotypes`

* Founders included: `r founders.included`

* CC Strains removed: `r strains.removed`

* Treatment of zeros: `r zero.text`

* Batch correction: `r correction.text`

* Normalization: `r norm.text`

```{r reload_data_none_rank_z}
if(cc.color.scheme == "original"){
    strain.colors <- CCorigcolors
}else{
    strain.colors <- CCcolors
}

cds <- read.csv(here("Data", paste0("CDS_", cds.file.date, ".csv")), strip.white = TRUE)
histopath <- read.csv(here("Data", paste0("histopath_", histopath.file.date, ".csv")), strip.white = TRUE)

#take out any slashes in strain names
cds[,"Strain"]  <- gsub("/", "", cds[,"Strain"], fixed = TRUE)
histopath[,"Strain"]  <- gsub("/", "", histopath[,"Strain"], fixed = TRUE)

#since Average Weight Loss was measured by simply taking the average of all the weight deltas weight loss is negative and gain is positive
#however this results in an inverted correlation and QTL plot between weight Loss and CDS despite both being metrics of disease severity
#below we can Loss average weight Loss to be negative and thus have CDS and Weight Loss correlate positively and map together
if(weight_loss){
  weight_data.text <- "Average Weight Loss reflects negative values for weight gain and positive values for weight loss"
  cds['Avg.Weight.Loss'] = cds['Avg.Weight.Loss'] *(-1)
}else{
  weight_data.text <- "Average Weight Loss reflects positive values for weight gain and negative values for weight loss."
}
```

```{r remove_strains_none_rank_z}
#remove any empty rows that were inserted to visually separate
#the founders from the rest of the strains
cds.data.idx <- which(!is.na(cds[,"ID"]))
cds <- cds[cds.data.idx,]

histopath.data.idx <- which(!is.na(histopath[,"ID"]))
histopath <- histopath[histopath.data.idx,]

#remove specified strains from cds
if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.cds <- unlist(sapply(remove_strains, function(x) which(cds[,"Strain"] == x)))
    cds <- cds[-remove.cds,]
}

if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.histopath <- unlist(sapply(remove_strains, function(x) which(histopath[,"Strain"] == x)))
    histopath <- histopath[histopath.data.idx,]
}
```


```{r batches_none_rank_z}
## CDS
num.cds <- as.matrix(sapply(cds[,c("Classical.CDS", "AR.CDS", "Global.CDS", "Avg.Weight.Loss")], as.numeric)) #had to change this with Chat to make the values numeric
rownames(num.cds) <- cds[,"ID"]
#set all scores equal to or lower than the disease threshold
#to zero
if(exclude.zeros){
    num.cds[which(num.cds <= disease.thresh)] <- 0
}

factor.cds <- as.matrix(cds[,c("Strain", "Sex")])
u_batch <- unique(cds[,"EXP"])

#remove animals in batch 170 - these were validation, not part of the original experiment
to.remove = "X170"
keep.batch <- which(u_batch != "X170")
u_batch <- u_batch[keep.batch]

keep.idx <- which(cds[,"EXP"] != "X170")
cds <- cds[keep.idx,]
num.cds <- num.cds[keep.idx,]
factor.cds <- factor.cds[keep.idx,]

batch.mat <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(cds), ncol = length(u_batch))
colnames(batch.mat) <- u_batch
for(b in 1:ncol(batch.mat)){
    batch.mat[which(cds[,"EXP"] == u_batch[b]),b] <- 1
}
cds.covar <- as.matrix(cbind(batch.mat, cds[,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE]))

pheno.strains <- cds[,"Strain"]
eae.strains <- unique(pheno.strains)

## histopath
# saving original histopath
histopath_legacy <- histopath

# removing animals that don't yet have histopathology scores
histopath <- na.omit(histopath)
removed_strains_histopath <- setdiff(unique(cds$Strain),unique(histopath$Strain)) #seeing what strains histopathology still lacks

# continuing with variable creation like above
num.histopath <- as.matrix(sapply(histopath[,c("H.E.SC.Overall", "H.E.BN.Overall", "LFB.SC.Overall", "LFB.BN.Overall")], as.numeric)) #had to change this with Chat to make the values numeric
rownames(num.histopath) <- histopath[,"ID"]
#set all scores equal to or lower than the disease threshold
#to zero
if(exclude.zeros){
    num.histopath[which(num.histopath <= disease.thresh)] <- 0
}

factor.histopath <- as.matrix(histopath[,c("Strain", "Sex")])
u_batch_histopath <- unique(histopath[,"EXP"])

#remove animals in batch 170 - these were validation, not part of the original experiment
to.remove = "X170"
keep_histopath.batch_histopath <- which(u_batch_histopath != "X170")
u_batch_histopath <- u_batch_histopath[keep_histopath.batch_histopath]

keep_histopath.idx <- which(histopath[,"EXP"] != "X170")
histopath <- histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]
num.histopath <- num.histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]
factor.histopath <- factor.histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]

batch_histopath.mat <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(histopath), ncol = length(u_batch_histopath))
colnames(batch_histopath.mat) <- u_batch_histopath
for(b in 1:ncol(batch_histopath.mat)){
    batch_histopath.mat[which(histopath[,"EXP"] == u_batch_histopath[b]),b] <- 1
}
histopath.covar <- as.matrix(cbind(batch_histopath.mat, histopath[,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE]))

pheno_histopath.strains_histopath <- histopath[,"Strain"]
eae_histopath.strains_histopath <- unique(pheno_histopath.strains_histopath)

# getting a histopath subset index
histopath_strain_names = unique(histopath$Strain)
```

```{r adj_cds_none_rank_z}
#choose which phenotypes to batch
#correct using their indices.
cds.idx <- c(1,2,3,4)
adj.cds <- num.cds

if(batch_as_covar){
    adj.cds <- adjust(num.cds, cds.covar[,1:ncol(cds.covar)-1])
}
```

```{r adj_histopath_none_rank_z}
#choose which phenotypes to batch
#correct using their indices.
histopath.idx <- c(1,2,3,4)

adj.histopath <- num.histopath

if(batch_as_covar){
    adj.histopath <- adjust(num.histopath, histopath.covar[,1:ncol(histopath.covar)-1])
}

```

```{r adjust_age_cds_none_rank_z, eval = FALSE}
#adjust for age
for(i in 1:ncol(adj.cds)){
    if(exclude.zeros){
        nonzero.idx <- which(num.cds[,i] != 0)
    }else{
        nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.cds)
    }
    adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i] <- adjust(adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE],
        cds.covar[nonzero.idx,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE])
}

#write out the results of whatever adjustments we have done
write.table(adj.cds, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Adj_CDS", file_added_name, ".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)
```

```{r no_norm_cds_none_rank_z}
norm.cds <- adj.cds
colnames(norm.cds) <- gsub(".CDS", "", colnames(norm.cds)) #take off CDS in labels, to save room in plots

if(rank_norm){
    for(i in 1:ncol(norm.cds)){
        if(exclude.zeros){
            nonzero.idx <- which(num.cds[,i] != 0)
        }else{
            nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.cds)
        }
        norm.cds[nonzero.idx,i] <- rankZ(adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i])
    }
}
colnames(norm.cds) <- gsub(".CDS", "", colnames(norm.cds)) #take off CDS, to save room in plots

#write out normzliaed values
write.table(norm.cds, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Norm_CDS",file_added_name,".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)

```

```{r adjust_age_histopath_none_rank_z, eval = FALSE}
#adjust for age
for(i in 1:ncol(adj.histopath)){
    if(exclude.zeros){
        nonzero.idx <- which(num.histopath[,i] != 0)
    }else{
        nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.histopath)
    }
    adj.histopath[nonzero.idx,i] <- adjust(adj.histopath[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE],
        histopath.covar[nonzero.idx,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE])
}

#write out the results of whatever adjustments we have done
write.table(adj.histopath, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Adj_histopath",file_added_name,".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)


#colnames(norm.histopath) <- gsub(".histopath", "", colnames(norm.histopath)) #take off CDS in labels, to save room in plots; not relavent now
```

```{r norm_histopath_none_rank_z}
norm.histopath <- adj.histopath
if(rank_norm){
    for(i in 1:ncol(norm.histopath)){
        if(exclude.zeros){
            nonzero.idx <- which(num.histopath[,i] != 0)
        }else{
            nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.histopath)
        }
        norm.histopath[nonzero.idx,i] <- rankZ(adj.histopath[nonzero.idx,i])
    }
}
#colnames(norm.histopath) <- gsub(".histopath", "", colnames(norm.histopath)) #take off CDS in labels, to save room in plots; not relevant now

#write out normzliaed values
write.table(norm.histopath, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Norm_histopath",file_added_name, ".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)

```

```{r methods_none_rank_z}
if(add_founder_genotypes){
    methods.line <- "Both CC and founder strains were used for mapping."
}else{
    methods.line <- "We used only CC strains for mapping."
}

if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.line <- paste("We excluded the following strains:", paste(remove_strains, collapse = ", "))
}else{
    remove.line <- "No CC strains were excluded."
}

if(length(removed_strains_histopath) !=0){
  remove.line <- paste("The strains that have CDS data but not yet histopathology data are:", paste(removed_strains_histopath, collapse = ", "))
}else{
  remove.line <- "All strains have both CDS and histopathology data."
}

#making histopath properly dimensioned for expanded matrices
if (!add_founder_genotypes){
  histopath_mod_legacy <- histopath_legacy[!(histopath_legacy$Strain %in% c('NOD','129')),] #didn't remove B6 because these are still used for other things
}
founder_ids <-
if (!add_founder_genotypes){
  norm.histopath <- norm.histopath[!(histopath$Strain %in% c('C57BL6','NOD','129')),]
}

```

```{r expand_matrices_cds_none_rank_z}

#remove any strains not used in mapping
mapping.strain.idx <- unlist(sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, pheno.strains)))
pheno.strains_cds <- pheno.strains[mapping.strain.idx]

#make a unique ID for each mouse that contains its strain and sex
u_id <- as.vector(apply(cds[mapping.strain.idx,c(1:2,5)], 1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = "_")))

K.idx <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, rownames(mapping.K[[1]])))
K.num <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) length(which(pheno.strains_cds == x)))
expanded.idx <- unlist(lapply(1:length(K.idx), function(x) rep(K.idx[x], K.num[x])))
groups <- names(expanded.idx)

exp.K <- lapply(1:length(mapping.K), function(x) mapping.K[[x]][expanded.idx,expanded.idx])
names(exp.K) <- names(mapping.K)

#create unique row and column names
for(i in 1:length(exp.K)){
    rownames(exp.K[[i]]) <- colnames(exp.K[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id)
}

expanded.genoprobs <- mapping.genoprobs
for(i in 1:length(expanded.genoprobs)){
    expanded.genoprobs[[i]] <- mapping.genoprobs[[i]][expanded.idx,,]
    rownames(expanded.genoprobs[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id)
}

#align the phenotypes with the strains
covar <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(cds[mapping.strain.idx,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(covar) <- 1:length(u_id)

mapping.pheno <- norm.cds[mapping.strain.idx,]
rownames(mapping.pheno) <- 1:length(u_id)

mapping.factors <- factor.cds[mapping.strain.idx,]
mapping.covar <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(mapping.factors[,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(mapping.covar) <- 1:length(u_id)
```

```{r expand_matrices_histopath_none_rank_z}

#remove any strains not used in mapping
mapping.strain.idx_histopath <- unlist(sapply(mapping.strains[histopath_mapping_idx], function(x) grep(x, pheno_histopath.strains_histopath)))
pheno.strains_histopath <- pheno_histopath.strains_histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath]

#make a unique ID for each mouse that contains its strain and sex
u_id_histopath <- as.vector(apply(histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath,c(1:2,5)],1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = "_")))

K.idx_histopath <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, rownames(mapping.K[[1]])))
K.num_histopath <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) length(which(pheno.strains_histopath == x)))
expanded.idx_histopath <- unlist(lapply(1:length(K.idx_histopath), function(x) rep(K.idx_histopath[x], K.num_histopath[x])))
groups_histopath <- names(expanded.idx_histopath)

exp.K_histopath <- lapply(1:length(mapping.K), function(x) mapping.K[[x]][expanded.idx_histopath,expanded.idx_histopath])
names(exp.K_histopath) <- names(mapping.K)

#create unique row and column names
for(i in 1:length(exp.K_histopath)){
    rownames(exp.K_histopath[[i]]) <- colnames(exp.K_histopath[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)
}

expanded.genoprobs_histopath <- mapping.genoprobs
for(i in 1:length(expanded.genoprobs_histopath)){
    expanded.genoprobs_histopath[[i]] <- mapping.genoprobs[[i]][expanded.idx_histopath,,]
    rownames(expanded.genoprobs_histopath[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)
}

#align the phenotypes with the strains
covar_histopath <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(covar_histopath) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)

mapping.pheno_histopath <- norm.histopath
rownames(mapping.pheno_histopath) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)

mapping.factors_histopath <- factor.histopath[mapping.strain.idx_histopath,]
mapping.covar_histopath <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(mapping.factors_histopath[,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(mapping.covar_histopath) <- 1:length(u_id_histopath)
```


```{r herit_cds_none_rank_z, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 5}
overall.k.file <- here("Data",paste0("kinship_overall_cds",file_added_name,".RDS"))
if(!file.exists(overall.k.file) || overwrite_previous){
    overall.K <- calc_kinship(expanded.genoprobs, "overall")
    saveRDS(overall.K, overall.k.file)
}else{
    overall.K <- readRDS(overall.k.file)
}

ind.herit <- rep(NA, ncol(num.cds))
names(ind.herit) <- colnames(num.cds)
for(i in 1:ncol(num.cds)){
    nonzero.idx <- which(mapping.pheno[,i] != 0)
    ind.herit[i] <- est_herit(mapping.pheno[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE], overall.K)
}


```

```{r herit_histopath_none_rank_z, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 5}
overall_histopath.k.file <- here("Data", paste0("kinship_overall_histopath",file_added_name,".RDS"))
if(!file.exists(overall_histopath.k.file) || overwrite_previous){
    overall_histopath.K <- calc_kinship(expanded.genoprobs_histopath, "overall")
    saveRDS(overall_histopath.K, overall_histopath.k.file)
}else{
    overall_histopath.K <- readRDS(overall_histopath.k.file)
}

ind.herit_histopath <- rep(NA, ncol(num.histopath))
names(ind.herit_histopath) <- colnames(num.histopath)
for(i in 1:ncol(num.histopath)){
    nonzero.idx <- which(mapping.pheno[,i] != 0)
    ind.herit_histopath[i] <- est_herit(mapping.pheno[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE], overall_histopath.K)
}


```

```{r map_cds_none_rank_z, warning = FALSE}

cds.scans <- map_pheno(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, exp.K,
    addcovar = covar, exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros,results.file = file.path(results.dir, paste0("scan1_results_cds",file_added_name,".RDS")))



if(run_permutations){

        exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros, nperm = nperm,

}
```

```{r map_histopath_none_rank_z, warning=FALSE}
        exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros, nperm = nperm,

}
```

#### CDS by individual LOD traces for None, Rank Z {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r plot_cds_scans_none_rank_z, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4, warning = FALSE, results = "asis"}
#plot the scans, and FDR results
if(run_permutations){
    #pdf("~/Desktop/test.pdf")
    fdr_summaries_cds <- plot_fdr_fun(scan.results = cds.scans, perm.results = cds.perm,
        tab.level = "#####", maxy = NULL)
    #sig_summaries_cds <- plot_pval_fun(scan.results = cds.scans, perm.results = cds.perm, tab.level = "####")
    #dev.off()
}else{
    for(i in 1:length(cds.scans)){
        cat("#####", names(cds.scans)[i], "\n")
        plot(cds.scans[[i]], map = map)
        cat("\n\n")
    }
}
```

#### Histopathology by individual LOD traces for None, Rank Z {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r plot_histopath_scans_none_rank_z, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4, warning = FALSE, results = "asis"}
#plot the scans, and FDR results
if(run_permutations){
    #pdf("~/Desktop/test.pdf")
    fdr_summaries_histopath <- plot_fdr_fun(scan.results = histopath.scans, perm.results = histopath.perm,
        tab.level = "#####", maxy = NULL)

}else{

        cat("#####", names(histopath.scans)[i], "\n")
```

#### LOD intervals for None, Rank Z {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r lod_peaks_cds_none_rank_z, results = "asis"}
top.n <- 3
for(ph in 1:length(cds.scans)){
    cat("#####", names(cds.scans)[ph], "\n")
    peak.table <- find_peaks(cds.scans[[ph]], map = map, prob = 0.95)
    lod.order <- order(peak.table[,"lod"], decreasing = TRUE)
    top.peaks <- peak.table[lod.order[1:top.n],3:ncol(peak.table)]
    rownames(top.peaks) <- NULL
    print(kable(top.peaks))
    cat("Thresholds:\n")
    print(kable(fdr_summaries_cds[ph,,drop=FALSE]))
    cat("\n\n")
}
```


```{r lod_peaks_histopath_none_rank_z, results = "asis"}
top.n <- 3
for(ph in 1:length(histopath.scans)){
    cat("#####", names(histopath.scans)[ph], "\n")
    peak.table_histopath <- find_peaks(histopath.scans[[ph]], map = map, prob = 0.95)
    lod.order_histopath <- order(peak.table_histopath[,"lod"], decreasing = TRUE)
    top.peaks_histopath <- peak.table_histopath[lod.order_histopath[1:top.n],3:ncol(peak.table_histopath)]
    rownames(top.peaks_histopath) <- NULL
    print(kable(top.peaks_histopath))
    cat("Thresholds:\n")
    print(kable(fdr_summaries_histopath[ph,,drop=FALSE]))
    cat("\n\n")
}
```

### B6, Rank Z

```{r parameters_and_start_up_B6_rank_z}

# changing name for new set up
file_added_name <- "_B6_rank_z"

# Using the flexibility from the previous code, these parameters will be updated for the none, none run
final_param = TRUE

if(final_param){
    b6_correction <- TRUE
    batch_as_covar <- FALSE
    rank_norm <- TRUE
    log_trans <- FALSE
    weight_loss <- TRUE
}
#===============================================================

if(is.na(b6_correction) || length(b6_correction) == 0){
b6_correction = FALSE; batch_as_covar = FALSE; rank_norm = FALSE
}

if(b6_correction && batch_as_covar){stop("Set either b6_correction or batch_as_covar to TRUE, not both.")}

alpha <- c(0.15, 0.2)

overwrite_previous <- FALSE # make this true whenever you add new data that you want fully analyzed; this WILL take longer and may not work on a low powered machine
add_founder_genotypes <- FALSE

#strains identified by remove_strains are excluded from all
#manipulations including normalization and batch corrections.
#justficiations are as follows:
#CC027 - all but 2 were homozygous for the wrong genotype at the H2 locus
#NOD and 129 - are not used for mapping, and were not included in all
#batches, so we cannot use them for normalization
#remove_strains <- NULL
remove_strains <- c("CC027", "NOD", "129")
#remove_strains <- c("CC051", "CC059") #closely related
#remove_strains <- c("CC051") #pick one of them to keep

cc.color.scheme = "original"
#cc.color.scheme = "new"

#for calculations
exclude.zeros = FALSE #if true, we exclude animals with a CDS of 0
disease.thresh = 0 #maximum disease score considered disease-free

nperm = 1000

if(nperm > 0){
    run_permutations <- TRUE
}else{
    run_permutations <- FALSE
}

if(add_founder_genotypes){
    founders.included = "yes"
}else{
    founders.included = "no"
}

if(length(remove_strains) > 0){
    strains.removed <- paste(remove_strains, collapse = ", ")
}else{
    strains.removed = "none"
}

if(!exclude.zeros){
    zero.text <- "Zeros are included in CDS"
    zero.treatment <- "included with"
}else{
    zero.text <- paste("Animals with scores of", disease.thresh, "and below are excluded from CDS.")
    zero.treatment <- "excluded from"
}

if(b6_correction){
    correction.text <- "Batch corrections were made using B6 CDS."
    correction.label <- "B6-batch"
}
if(batch_as_covar){
    correction.text <- "Batch corrections were done using batch as a covariate."
    correction.label <- "covar-batch"
}
if(!b6_correction && !batch_as_covar){
    correction.text <- "No batch corrections were done."
    correction.label <- "no-batch"
}

if(rank_norm){
    norm.text <- "Rank Z normalization was performed."
    norm.label <- "rank-norm"
}else if (log_trans){
    norm.text <- "Log Transformation was performed"
    norm.label <- 'log-trans'
}else{
    norm.text <- "Rank Z normalization was not performed."
    norm.label <- "no-norm"
}

file.label <- paste(correction.label, norm.label, genotypes, sep = "_")
```

The parameters for this run are as follows:

* Genotypes used: `r genotypes`

* Founders included: `r founders.included`

* CC Strains removed: `r strains.removed`

* Treatment of zeros: `r zero.text`

* Batch correction: `r correction.text`

* Normalization: `r norm.text`

```{r reload_data_B6_rank_z}
if(cc.color.scheme == "original"){
    strain.colors <- CCorigcolors
}else{
    strain.colors <- CCcolors
}

cds <- read.csv(here("Data", paste0("CDS_", cds.file.date, ".csv")), strip.white = TRUE)
histopath <- read.csv(here("Data", paste0("histopath_", histopath.file.date, ".csv")), strip.white = TRUE)

#take out any slashes in strain names
cds[,"Strain"]  <- gsub("/", "", cds[,"Strain"], fixed = TRUE)
histopath[,"Strain"]  <- gsub("/", "", histopath[,"Strain"], fixed = TRUE)

#since Average Weight Loss was measured by simply taking the average of all the weight deltas weight loss is negative and gain is positive
#however this results in an inverted correlation and QTL plot between weight Loss and CDS despite both being metrics of disease severity
#below we can Loss average weight Loss to be negative and thus have CDS and Weight Loss correlate positively and map together
if(weight_loss){
  weight_data.text <- "Average Weight Loss reflects negative values for weight gain and positive values for weight loss"
  cds['Avg.Weight.Loss'] = cds['Avg.Weight.Loss'] *(-1)
}else{
  weight_data.text <- "Average Weight Loss reflects positive values for weight gain and negative values for weight loss."
}
```

This run uses the CDS and Histopathology file from `r gsub("_", " ", cds.file.date)` and `r gsub("_", " ", histopath.file.date)`.

If any strains need to be removed from the analysis, do
that now before any numbers are manipulated.

Since not all of the mice have histopathology scores yet, rows with no data are omitted from the histopathology matrix. Once new scores are added to the data set, these strains should repopulate.

```{r remove_strains_B6_rank_z}
#remove any empty rows that were inserted to visually separate
#the founders from the rest of the strains
cds.data.idx <- which(!is.na(cds[,"ID"]))
cds <- cds[cds.data.idx,]

histopath.data.idx <- which(!is.na(histopath[,"ID"]))
histopath <- histopath[histopath.data.idx,]

#remove specified strains from cds
if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.cds <- unlist(sapply(remove_strains, function(x) which(cds[,"Strain"] == x)))
    cds <- cds[-remove.cds,]
}

if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.histopath <- unlist(sapply(remove_strains, function(x) which(histopath[,"Strain"] == x)))
    histopath <- histopath[histopath.data.idx,]
}
```


```{r batches_B6_rank_z}
## CDS
num.cds <- as.matrix(sapply(cds[,c("Classical.CDS", "AR.CDS", "Global.CDS", "Avg.Weight.Loss")], as.numeric)) #had to change this with Chat to make the values numeric
rownames(num.cds) <- cds[,"ID"]
#set all scores equal to or lower than the disease threshold
#to zero
if(exclude.zeros){
    num.cds[which(num.cds <= disease.thresh)] <- 0
}

factor.cds <- as.matrix(cds[,c("Strain", "Sex")])
u_batch <- unique(cds[,"EXP"])

#remove animals in batch 170 - these were validation, not part of the original experiment
to.remove = "X170"
keep.batch <- which(u_batch != "X170")
u_batch <- u_batch[keep.batch]

keep.idx <- which(cds[,"EXP"] != "X170")
cds <- cds[keep.idx,]
num.cds <- num.cds[keep.idx,]
factor.cds <- factor.cds[keep.idx,]

batch.mat <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(cds), ncol = length(u_batch))
colnames(batch.mat) <- u_batch
for(b in 1:ncol(batch.mat)){
    batch.mat[which(cds[,"EXP"] == u_batch[b]),b] <- 1
}
cds.covar <- as.matrix(cbind(batch.mat, cds[,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE]))

pheno.strains <- cds[,"Strain"]
eae.strains <- unique(pheno.strains)

## histopath
# saving original histopath
histopath_legacy <- histopath

# removing animals that don't yet have histopathology scores
histopath <- na.omit(histopath)
removed_strains_histopath <- setdiff(unique(cds$Strain),unique(histopath$Strain)) #seeing what strains histopathology still lacks

# continuing with variable creation like above
num.histopath <- as.matrix(sapply(histopath[,c("H.E.SC.Overall", "H.E.BN.Overall", "LFB.SC.Overall", "LFB.BN.Overall")], as.numeric)) #had to change this with Chat to make the values numeric
rownames(num.histopath) <- histopath[,"ID"]
#set all scores equal to or lower than the disease threshold
#to zero
if(exclude.zeros){
    num.histopath[which(num.histopath <= disease.thresh)] <- 0
}

factor.histopath <- as.matrix(histopath[,c("Strain", "Sex")])
u_batch_histopath <- unique(histopath[,"EXP"])

#remove animals in batch 170 - these were validation, not part of the original experiment
to.remove = "X170"
keep_histopath.batch_histopath <- which(u_batch_histopath != "X170")
u_batch_histopath <- u_batch_histopath[keep_histopath.batch_histopath]

keep_histopath.idx <- which(histopath[,"EXP"] != "X170")
histopath <- histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]
num.histopath <- num.histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]
factor.histopath <- factor.histopath[keep_histopath.idx,]

batch_histopath.mat <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(histopath), ncol = length(u_batch_histopath))
colnames(batch_histopath.mat) <- u_batch_histopath
for(b in 1:ncol(batch_histopath.mat)){
    batch_histopath.mat[which(histopath[,"EXP"] == u_batch_histopath[b]),b] <- 1
}
histopath.covar <- as.matrix(cbind(batch_histopath.mat, histopath[,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE]))

pheno_histopath.strains_histopath <- histopath[,"Strain"]
eae_histopath.strains_histopath <- unique(pheno_histopath.strains_histopath)

# getting a histopath subset index
histopath_strain_names = unique(histopath$Strain)
```

#### Batch correction procedure

No batch correction was performed for this run.

The B6 batch correction procedure is as follows:

1. Calculate the overall mean CDS for B6 mice
    across the entire experiment
2. Calculate the mean CDS B6 mice in each batch.
3. Adjust all mice in each batch based on the
    difference in means in the B6. Subtract off
    the mean deviation, and divide by the standard
    deviation.
4. Male and female B6 mice do not have a sex
    difference in CDS, and adjusting by the
    two sexes separately could inadvertently
    introduce a sex effect into the other strains,
    so we use overall means rather than sex-specific
    means.

The batch correction procedure using batch as a covariate is as follows:

1. Create a binary matrix indicating whether each individual
    was run in each batch (1) or not (0).
2. Regress this matrix out of the CDS matrix.

```{r adj_cds_B6_rank_z}

b6.idx <- which(factor.cds[,1] == "C57BL6")

if(exclude.zeros){
    nonzero.idx <- lapply(1:ncol(num.cds), function(x) which(num.cds[,x] != 0))
}else{
    nonzero.idx <- lapply(1:ncol(num.cds), function(x) 1:nrow(num.cds))
}

b6.batch.idx <- apply(cds.covar[,1:(ncol(cds.covar)-1)], 2, 
    function(x) intersect(which(x == 1), b6.idx))

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
b6.by.batch <- vector(mode = "list", length = ncol(num.cds))
names(b6.by.batch) <- colnames(num.cds)
for(cd in 1:ncol(num.cds)){
    #get batch values for this CDS
    vals.by.batch <- lapply(1:length(b6.batch.idx), 
        function(x) num.cds[intersect(b6.batch.idx[[x]], nonzero.idx[[cd]]),cd])
    names(vals.by.batch) <- u_batch
    b6.by.batch[[cd]] <- vals.by.batch
    boxplot(b6.by.batch[[cd]], main = colnames(num.cds)[cd])    
}

#choose which phenotypes to batch
#correct using their indices.
cds.idx <- c(1,2,3,4)
adj.cds <- num.cds

if(batch_as_covar){
    adj.cds <- adjust(num.cds, cds.covar[,1:ncol(cds.covar)-1])
}

if(b6_correction){
    batch.mean <- sapply(b6.by.batch, function(x) sapply(x, mean))
    batch.sd <- sapply(b6.by.batch, function(x) sapply(x, sd))
    batch.sd[which(is.na(batch.sd))] <- 1

    #for each batch
    for(b in c(1:(ncol(cds.covar)-1))){
        batch.idx <- which(cds.covar[,b] == 1)
        for(j in cds.idx){ #only correct classical and global
            if(exclude.zeros){
                    nonzero.idx <- which(num.cds[,j] != 0)
                }else{
                    nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.cds)
                }
            adj.idx <- intersect(batch.idx, nonzero.idx)
            adj.cds[adj.idx,j] <- (num.cds[adj.idx,j] - batch.mean[b,j])/batch.sd[b,j]
        }
    }

}
```

```{r adjust_age_cds_B6_rank_z, eval = FALSE}
#adjust for age
for(i in 1:ncol(adj.cds)){
    if(exclude.zeros){
        nonzero.idx <- which(num.cds[,i] != 0)
    }else{
        nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.cds)
    }
    adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i] <- adjust(adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE],
        cds.covar[nonzero.idx,"Age.at.D0",drop=FALSE])
}

#write out the results of whatever adjustments we have done
write.table(adj.cds, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Adj_CDS",file_added_name,".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)
```

No normalization for this run.

```{r no_norm_cds_B6_rank_z}
norm.cds <- adj.cds
colnames(norm.cds) <- gsub(".CDS", "", colnames(norm.cds)) #take off CDS in labels, to save room in plots

if(rank_norm){
    for(i in 1:ncol(norm.cds)){
        if(exclude.zeros){
            nonzero.idx <- which(num.cds[,i] != 0)
        }else{
            nonzero.idx <- 1:nrow(num.cds)
        }
        norm.cds[nonzero.idx,i] <- rankZ(adj.cds[nonzero.idx,i])
    }
}
colnames(norm.cds) <- gsub(".CDS", "", colnames(norm.cds)) #take off CDS, to save room in plots

#write out normzliaed values
write.table(norm.cds, file.path(results.dir, paste0("Norm_CDS",file_added_name,".csv")), sep = ",", quote = FALSE)

```

#### Mapping with Individuals

```{r methods_B6_rank_z}
if(add_founder_genotypes){
    methods.line <- "Both CC and founder strains were used for mapping."
}else{
    methods.line <- "We used only CC strains for mapping."
}

if(length(remove_strains) != 0){
    remove.line <- paste("We excluded the following strains:", paste(remove_strains, collapse = ", "))
}else{
    remove.line <- "No CC strains were excluded."
}

if(length(removed_strains_histopath) !=0){
  remove.line <- paste("The strains that have CDS data but not yet histopathology data are:", paste(removed_strains_histopath, collapse = ", "))
}else{
  remove.line <- "All strains have both CDS and histopathology data."
}

# #making histopath properly dimensioned for expanded matrices
# if (!add_founder_genotypes){
#   histopath_mod_legacy <- histopath_legacy[!(histopath_legacy$Strain %in% c('NOD','129')),] #didn't remove B6 because these are still used for other things
# }
# founder_ids <-
# if (!add_founder_genotypes){
#   norm.histopath <- norm.histopath[!(histopath$Strain %in% c('C57BL6','NOD','129')),]
# }

```

`r methods.line`
`r remove.line`

For the genoprobs and kinship matrices, we copied
the entries for the individuals in each strain and
used indices for row names. CC genotype data not dependent on either CDS or histopathology was no re-calculated.

The LOD traces for each trait are shown below along
with plots for the FDR results. Significance thresholds
from the permutations are also shown on the LOD traces.

```{r expand_matrices_cds_B6_rank_z}

#remove any strains not used in mapping
mapping.strain.idx <- unlist(sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, pheno.strains)))
pheno.strains_cds <- pheno.strains[mapping.strain.idx]

#make a unique ID for each mouse that contains its strain and sex
u_id <- as.vector(apply(cds[mapping.strain.idx,c(1:2,5)], 1, function(x) paste(x, collapse = "_")))

K.idx <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) grep(x, rownames(mapping.K[[1]])))
K.num <- sapply(mapping.strains, function(x) length(which(pheno.strains_cds == x)))
expanded.idx <- unlist(lapply(1:length(K.idx), function(x) rep(K.idx[x], K.num[x])))
groups <- names(expanded.idx)

exp.K <- lapply(1:length(mapping.K), function(x) mapping.K[[x]][expanded.idx,expanded.idx])
names(exp.K) <- names(mapping.K)

#create unique row and column names
for(i in 1:length(exp.K)){
    rownames(exp.K[[i]]) <- colnames(exp.K[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id)
}

expanded.genoprobs <- mapping.genoprobs
for(i in 1:length(expanded.genoprobs)){
    expanded.genoprobs[[i]] <- mapping.genoprobs[[i]][expanded.idx,,]
    rownames(expanded.genoprobs[[i]]) <- 1:length(u_id)
}

#align the phenotypes with the strains
covar <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(cds[mapping.strain.idx,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(covar) <- 1:length(u_id)

mapping.pheno <- norm.cds[mapping.strain.idx,]
rownames(mapping.pheno) <- 1:length(u_id)

mapping.factors <- factor.cds[mapping.strain.idx,]
mapping.covar <- matrix(as.numeric(as.factor(mapping.factors[,"Sex"])), ncol = 1)
rownames(mapping.covar) <- 1:length(u_id)
```

The heritability was re-calculated and new data was saved to new files.

```{r herit_cds_B6_rank_z, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 5}
overall.k.file <- here("Data", paste0("kinship_overall_cds",file_added_name,".RDS"))
if(!file.exists(overall.k.file) || overwrite_previous){
    overall.K <- calc_kinship(expanded.genoprobs, "overall")
    saveRDS(overall.K, overall.k.file)
}else{
    overall.K <- readRDS(overall.k.file)
}

ind.herit <- rep(NA, ncol(num.cds))
names(ind.herit) <- colnames(num.cds)
for(i in 1:ncol(num.cds)){
    nonzero.idx <- which(mapping.pheno[,i] != 0)
    ind.herit[i] <- est_herit(mapping.pheno[nonzero.idx,i,drop=FALSE], overall.K)
}


```

```{r map_cds_B6_rank_z, warning = FALSE}

cds.scans <- map_pheno(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, exp.K,
    addcovar = covar, exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros,results.file = file.path(results.dir, paste0("scan1_results_cds",file_added_name,".RDS")))



if(run_permutations){
    #perm_strata <- names(expanded.idx)
    #names(perm_strata) <- rownames(mapping.pheno)
    #cds.perm <- scan1perm(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, kinship = exp.K,
    #    addcovar = covar, perm_strata = perm_strata, n_perm = nperm, cores = detectCores()-1)

    #cds.perm <- scan1perm(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, kinship = exp.K,
    #    addcovar = covar, n_perm = nperm, cores = detectCores()-1)

    cds.perm <- perm_scans(expanded.genoprobs, mapping.pheno, exp.K,
        cds.scans, addcovar = covar, groups = names(expanded.idx),
        exclude.zeros = exclude.zeros, nperm = nperm,
        results.file = file.path(results.dir, paste0("scan1perm_results_cds_perm_scans",file_added_name,".RDS")))
}
```

#### CDS by individual LOD traces for B6, Rank Z {.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

```{r plot_cds_scans_B6_rank_z, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 4, warning = FALSE, results = "asis"}
#plot the scans, and FDR results
if(run_permutations){
    #pdf("~/Desktop/test.pdf")
    fdr_summaries_cds <- plot_fdr_fun(scan.results = cds.scans, perm.results = cds.perm,
        tab.level = "#####", maxy = NULL)
    #sig_summaries_cds <- plot_pval_fun(scan.results = cds.scans, perm.results = cds.perm, tab.level = "####")
    #dev.off()
}else{
    for(i in 1:length(cds.scans)){
        cat("#####", names(cds.scans)[i], "\n")
        plot(cds.scans[[i]], map = map)
        cat("\n\n")
    }
}
```

#### LOD intervals B6, Rank Z{.tabset .tabset-fade .tabset-pills}

The following tables show LOD peak intervals for the top 3 peaks from
each trait.

```{r lod_peaks_cds_B6_rank_z, results = "asis"}
top.n <- 3
for(ph in 1:length(cds.scans)){
    cat("#####", names(cds.scans)[ph], "\n")
    peak.table <- find_peaks(cds.scans[[ph]], map = map, prob = 0.95)
    lod.order <- order(peak.table[,"lod"], decreasing = TRUE)
    top.peaks <- peak.table[lod.order[1:top.n],3:ncol(peak.table)]
    rownames(top.peaks) <- NULL
    print(kable(top.peaks))
    cat("Thresholds:\n")
    print(kable(fdr_summaries_cds[ph,,drop=FALSE]))
    cat("\n\n")
}
```

```{r lod_peaks_histopath_B6_rank_z, results = "asis"}
#     lod.order_histopath <- order(peak.table_histopath[,"lod"], decreasing = TRUE)
```
